hinge: Axial CBCT sections reveal the number of remaining bony walls in infrabony defects. A three-wall defect has three intact osseous walls and carries the best regenerative prognosis among infrabony defect morphologies.

Koilonychia (spoon nails), smooth red tongue, pallor, jaundice, and microcytic anemia (low RBC and low MCV) are classic for iron-deficiency anemia. Pernicious anemia is macrocytic with high MCV from B12 deficiency.

Severe xerostomia from minor salivary gland disease makes mucosa-borne RPDs poorly retained and traumatic. Implant-supported fixed partial denture avoids reliance on saliva for retention and is the preferred option when adequate bone exists.

Sarcoidosis on minor salivary gland biopsy shows noncaseating granulomatous inflammation as the diagnostic histopathologic pattern. Fibrosis, acanthosis, and acinar hypertrophy are not the defining histologic feature.

Before taking a definitive impression for a new RPD, mucosa compressed by the old prosthesis needs rest to rebound. Leaving the old prosthesis out approximately 24 hours is the standard tissue rest interval.
Maxillary major connectors should clear the free gingival margins by approximately 6 mm to prevent plaque trapping and gingival inflammation. Mandibular connectors require about 3 mm clearance.

Myxedema coma is severe decompensated hypothyroidism with altered mental status. Hypothermia is the cardinal early sign from failed thermoregulation, preceding bradycardia and other systemic decompensation.

After scaling and root planing, inflamed connective tissue and long junctional epithelium remodel: inflammatory infiltrate first regresses as healing begins, then collagen organization and attachment quality increase during the maturation phase of periodontal wound healing.

A female patient with an inhaler who suddenly becomes aggressive during dental treatment most likely has hyperventilation syndrome — anxiety-driven tachypnea causing respiratory alkalosis with altered mental status. Hypoglycemia lacks the inhaler context and requires a diabetic history not mentioned in the stem.

Cavit is a zinc oxide-based temporary endodontic provisional material only. It lacks the strength, long-term seal, and wear resistance for definitive restoration and must be replaced with a proper temporary or permanent material.

A primary upper anterior tooth that darkens after trauma then spontaneously lightens suggests transient pulp hemorrhage with resorption of blood pigments and recovering vitality. Observation and follow-up with vitality testing is the appropriate management.

When infective endocarditis prophylaxis is indicated and the patient cannot take oral medications, parenteral ampicillin (IM/IV, typically 2 g) is the standard substitute. Oral amoxicillin, azithromycin, and clindamycin apply only when oral dosing is possible.

Rigidly connecting a natural tooth to an implant creates differential mobility — the tooth's PDL allows micro-movement while the implant is rigidly osseointegrated. This tends to break the cement seal on the tooth abutment rather than equally stressing both units.

Multiple caries and abscesses in a young child define severe early childhood caries as the clinical dental diagnosis. While neglect may be a social risk factor, the question asks for the disease diagnosis, not the type of maltreatment.

Toughness is the energy absorbed per unit volume up to fracture — the area under the complete stress-strain curve. Modulus of elasticity is stiffness (slope of the elastic portion), not total energy to failure.

A plaque index of 40% with BOP of 20% indicates substantial plaque remains despite some cleaning effort with moderate inflammation. This pattern matches inadequate brushing technique with attempted oral hygiene rather than total non-brushing.

Chronic mouth breathing is often associated with maxillary constriction and narrow arch form. Orthopedic maxillary expansion improves the nasal airway space and transverse arch dimension as the primary skeletal/orthodontic treatment approach.

This item is poorly formed with garbled options. HBV is a blood-borne pathogen transmitted via percutaneous/mucosal exposure, not respiratory droplets. The keyed option appears to be the only HBV-related placeholder choice in this item.

Carbohydrate-driven demineralization under or around a prosthesis leads to white-spot lesions and caries. Caries is the pathologic process; dietary investigation is an adjunctive history step, not the lesion diagnosis itself.

Porcelain is brittle under tension but relatively strong under compression. PFM design places porcelain under compressive loading in function through proper metal substructure support and coping geometry to minimize tensile and shear stresses that initiate ceramic fracture.

For traumatic loss of an upper central incisor (#21) in an athletic patient, a bone-level implant allows platform placement for optimal soft-tissue thickness and esthetic emergence profile in the high-smile anterior maxilla. Tissue-level designs are less flexible for esthetic zones.

Generalized brown intrinsic tooth discoloration that fluoresces yellow under ultraviolet light is classic for tetracycline staining incorporated during tooth formation. Extrinsic stains and most other intrinsic defects lack this UV fluorescence pattern.

An intact restoration with tight contacts, normal probing depth, food impaction, and gingival pain suggests closed embrasures that trap food debris. This creates a local food impaction problem, not periodontal pocketing or plaque-induced disease.

Hormonal changes during the menstrual cycle increase gingival vascular permeability and crevicular fluid flow. This produces transient gingivitis-like signs without actual change in probing pocket depth from true attachment loss.

Enamel hypoplasia limited to incisal tips and cusp tips indicates a short systemic insult during early postnatal/perinatal crown formation — chronologic enamel hypoplasia from perinatal nutritional deficiency or illness. Fluorosis and amelogenesis imperfecta are generalized patterns.
The angle former is a hand instrument designed to sharpen internal line angles, place retention features in dentin, and place enamel bevels — particularly in cast gold cavity preparations.

Perineural invasion on histology of a salivary gland neoplasm is the hallmark of adenoid cystic carcinoma, explaining its characteristic pain and tendency for local spread along nerve pathways. Mucoepidermoid carcinoma more often shows cystic, mucous, and epidermoid cells without this classic perineural pattern.

Generalized hypocalcified enamel requires masking of color defects and structural reinforcement. Composite veneers restore esthetics and protect weakened enamel. Microabrasion and bleaching are inadequate alone for substantial hypocalcification.

Legal and clinical quality of dental records depends first on completeness: history, examination findings, diagnosis, informed consent, treatment performed, and follow-up must all be documented. Incomplete notes fail medico-legal and quality standards even if entries are objective and specific.

A nonvital tooth with an apical abscess and radiographic periapical pathosis represents primary endodontic disease. Primary periodontal disease would show deep pockets and crestal bone loss as the driver without a primary pulp source.
